raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact makes comparative and causal conclusions (e.g., 'gains stem primarily from the per-prompt quality classifier') that require checking internal logical coherence across premises and intermediates.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims are asserted (quality=0.84, 41% of frontier cost, 35% absolute quality improvement) without visible tables or experiment details in the artifact; verifying that these numbers are consistently grounded is central to any 'check'."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus primarily on verifying the quantitative claims and evidence-to-conclusion logic (R1.2/R1.3), or is this a writing and prose review (R1.6)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes comparative and causal conclusions (e.g., 'gains stem primarily from the per-prompt quality classifier') that require checking internal logical coherence across premises and intermediates.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (quality=0.84, 41% of frontier cost, 35% absolute quality improvement) without visible tables or experiment details in the artifact; verifying that these numbers are consistently grounded is central to any 'check'.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on verifying the quantitative claims and evidence-to-conclusion logic (R1.2/R1.3), or is this a writing and prose review (R1.6)?
